sections: HCM-1.4 — Profile-aware setup and doctor decisions
HCM-0.6 exact shipped kind and root-profile data
HCM-1.3 descriptor-driven artifact registry proof gate
HCM-1.4 profile-aware setup and doctor decision proof gate
reviewed dependency, implementation, and proof evidence

repo_truth: Fresh read-only review of the exact seven-file HCM-1.4 planning subject at entry HEAD 8e3af469. HCM-1.1 through HCM-1.3 are landed dependencies. This session is planning-only: no Rust/product implementation is authorized. Independently derive a verdict from live authority, source, tests, definitions, package manifests, and Git history; inherit no parent conclusion.
allowed_scope: Remain read-only and replay the sorted seven-entry manifest and aggregate fingerprint before review.
Review every byte of 00, 04, 06, the HCM-1.4 SPEC, plan, unchecked todo, and planning proof wall.

Read unchanged control-pack authority, HCM-1.1/HCM-1.2/HCM-1.3 packets and closeouts, live setup/doctor/profile/registry source and tests, definition assets, package manifests, and official dependency documentation only as needed.
Run read-only Markdown, link, Git, handoff/schema, API feasibility, scope-allowlist, TDD-ordering, package-boundary, compatibility, determinism, and security checks.
Return findings first with exact file/line evidence through the built-in channel.
non_goals: No edits, staging, commits, new dispatches, global handoffs, ledger writes, or user task hops.
No Rust, Cargo, runtime/product, canonical content, authoring, flow, HCM-2+, SDK, Tauri, Substrate, adapter, contract, or dock implementation.
Do not infer an unspecified schema, identity, fingerprint, default, compatibility, migration, fallback, CLI input, machine DTO envelope, content authority, or later-slice behavior.
Do not treat the parent proof-wall pre-review verdict as approval.
tasks: Invoke required skills in order, remain read-only, replay the exact seven-entry manifest/aggregate, and return BLOCKED if any byte differs.

Review findings first in Critical, Required, Optional, Nit order. Every actionable finding must include severity, exact file/line, violated authority/invariant/gate, concrete failure, smallest valid remediation, and invalidated proof.
Verify planning-only status, exact HCM-1.3 dependency/selector, packet completeness, unchecked todo, two-commit closeout, and no implementation or HCM-2 activation.

Verify every frozen public API/type/error/status/algorithm is feasible against live HCM-1.2/HCM-1.3 owner APIs and setup/doctor/CLI call structure without inventing a forbidden compatibility bridge or broader authority.

Verify setup and doctor genuinely consume one selected-profile registry/decision closure, fixed artifact decision symbols are retired only for those seams, and sibling fixed surfaces remain explicitly owned and unchanged.

Verify all six condition outcomes, precedence, evidence/fingerprint/source/self-reference rules, empty-input behavior, determinism, and false-from-silence protections against exact control-pack definitions.
Verify structural read-only inspection and setup no-write/rewrite-refusal/reset-atomicity contracts do not steal HCM-2 content authority and include realistic cross-platform path/race/bounds tests.
Verify human wording/CLI ownership, machine truth/status precedence, no new CLI grammar or final HCM-4 DTO overclaim, and text/JSON/exit consistency.
Verify exact existing/new file allowlists cover every named implementation/test/proof/first-commit/second-commit task, no necessary file is forbidden, and scope stops are enforceable.

Verify genuine RED-before-production TDD order, shipped/custom positive proof, negative/security/determinism regressions, fixed-symbol absence, package member set/hash/size equality, Windows, GitNexus, scope, secret, whitespace, and handoff proof.
Verify classification ceiling and open gates match phase authority, and the future implementation is separable without inventing a decision.
Return CLEAN only if there is no valid Critical or Required finding; otherwise return CHANGES_REQUIRED or BLOCKED. Make no edits.
stop_conditions: Return BLOCKED if the manifest/aggregate, required authority, live source, or mandatory read-only verification cannot be reproduced.
Return CHANGES_REQUIRED rather than filling a missing schema, identity, fingerprint, compatibility, CLI input, content-authority, or later-slice decision by inference.
Return to the parent if remediation would require Rust/runtime work, broader control-pack authority, HCM-2+, or global handoff/ledger mutation.
